channel.title: fuzz-testing on ₿itcoin Transcripts
channel.description: Recent content in fuzz-testing on ₿itcoin Transcripts

item.title: Fuzzamoto
item.description: See also: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1wR7ln1Seoonf--ocZVIUgihVFj5EEx7JJ3g_6ZdzVa4/edit?usp=sharing Basics https://github.com/dergoegge/fuzzamoto More detailed docs: https://dergoegge.github.io/fuzzamoto/ Fuzzing but at the same level as the functional tests, i.e. testing happens through rpc/p2p/ipc Full system snapshot fuzzing Workflow: start the test spawn one/multiple bitcoinds and generate state create snapshot for each fuzzing iterations the state resets to the snapshot The snapshots are important for determinism. The fuzzamoto approach can find crashes, hangs, consensus bugs, … Pretty much any of the properties we care about.
